what-delete-does.label: What DELETE does
what-delete-does.summary: Understand the purpose and effects of the SQL DELETE statement, including how it removes rows from a table and why careful targeting is essential.

code_2.title: Delete a specific test item by name

code_3.title: Delete all items in the 'Decor' category

multi-1.title: Effects of DELETE without WHERE
multi-1.prompt: Which of the following statements about DELETE without a WHERE clause are true? Select all that apply.
multi-1.hint: Think about what happens if you don't filter the rows to delete.

help.concept: A DELETE statement without a WHERE clause removes every row from the table.
help.hint_1: Consider the scope of DELETE when no condition is set.
help.hint_2: Does it affect one row, some rows, or all rows?

options.a: a. All rows in the table will be deleted.
options.b: b. The table structure remains, but it becomes empty.
options.c: c. Only the first row will be deleted.
options.d: d. The table is dropped from the database.
options.e: e. You can recover the deleted rows using SELECT.

fill-1.title: Purpose of the WHERE clause in DELETE
fill-1.prompt: Choose the best value for the first missing blank in the statement.
fill-1.hint: Think about how SQL decides which rows to delete.

help.concept: The WHERE clause specifies the condition that rows must meet to be deleted.
help.hint_1: It helps SQL know which rows to target.
help.hint_2: Without it, all rows are deleted.

sketch-1.title: What the DELETE Statement Does
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
The SQL `DELETE` statement is used to remove rows from a table. When you run a `DELETE` command, the specified rows are permanently removed from the table—they cannot be recovered unless you have a backup. 

For example, to delete all test items from the `inventory_items` table:

```sql
DELETE FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

This command deletes every row where the `is_test` column is set to 1. If you omit the `WHERE` clause, **all rows in the table will be deleted**. Always double-check your `WHERE` condition before running a `DELETE` statement.
